navSteps: ENVIRONMENT REQUIREMENT: This spec requires NEXT_PUBLIC_INVENTORY_ENABLED to be unset or =0 in .env.local, since when it is =1 the /purchases route redirects to /supplies (which shows inventory items, not purchase orders with editable line-item tables). Assuming the environment is configured correctly: 1. Navigate to /purchases (will not redirect if INVENTORY_ENABLED is off). 2. Wait for the page heading "Purchases" to appear. 3. Click the first h3 heading with text matching /^Order\s+/i (e.g., "Order DemoStrain ΔADE2 reagents") to expand that purchase order card. 4. Wait 900ms for the expansion animation. 5. The PurchaseEditor component will render the line-item table below the card header, showing columns: Item Name, Qty, Link, CAS / Accession, Price/Unit, Shipping, Total, Funding String, Vendor, Catalog #, Category, Notes, Assigned to, Order status, PI status. 6. Click on the first Vendor input field in the first row of the table to focus it. 7. Type "N" with a 100ms delay to trigger the vendor autocomplete datalist, surfacing options like "NEB", "Sigma-Aldrich", etc.
idealShot: An expanded purchase order (e.g., "Order DemoStrain ΔADE2 reagents") displayed on /purchases with the inline line-item table fully visible. The table should show all columns including Item Name, Qty, Vendor, CAS, Category, and other fields. The Vendor column in the first row should have focus and show an open autocomplete datalist with vendor suggestions (NEB, Sigma-Aldrich, etc.) visible beneath the input field. The table should display actual fixture data (real item names, prices, funding strings).

problem: image-annotation-gel.png has no capture spec. The PNG file does not exist at /Users/gnickles/Desktop/ResearchOS/frontend/public/wiki/screenshots/image-annotation-gel.png. The page /wiki/features/image-annotation references it but has only a TODO comment describing what should be captured.
navSteps: 1. Navigate to /experiments with the wiki-capture fixture loaded. 2. Click the Experiments tab if not already on it (Projects is the default). 3. Find and open the completed experiment task "Yeast transformation: pYES-GAL1::flbA" (alex's task 2) by clicking its card. 4. Click into the Lab Notes tab inside the task popup. 5. Switch to Preview mode using the editor mode toggle (if in Edit mode). 6. Locate the task image (look for any image with alt-text or the cursor-pointer class) in the task description. 7. Click the image to open the ImageResizePopover. 8. Click the "Annotate" button (pencil icon) in the popover to open the full-screen ImageAnnotatorModal. 9. Use the toolbar to draw annotations: (a) Click the Ellipse tool and draw a red ellipse around a gel band (use the red color from COLORS[0]). (b) Click the Arrow tool and draw a blue arrow pointing at a feature (switch color to COLORS[3] blue). (c) Click the Text tool and place text labels "Lane 1", "Lane 2", "Lane 3" across the top of the gel using a smaller font size (18 or 28px). Do not save.
fixtureNeed: The wiki-capture fixture must seed a gel image (at least 600x400px, typically a gel electrophoresis photo) in the fixture task's Images folder so it loads in the editor. Without a seeded image file in the fixture, the ImageAnnotatorModal will fail to load. The capture action can draw the annotations on any image, but a gel photo makes the demo most realistic per the page's example caption.

problem: The current spec tries to click a toolbar "Primer" button that no longer exists. According to the code (SequenceEditView.tsx lines 5048-5056), the Feature/Primer/Enzyme toolbar dropdowns were removed and replaced with right-click context menus. The screenshot currently shows the sequences library interface, not the Add Primer dialog.
navSteps: 1. Wait for the sequence pEGFP-N1 to load in edit mode. 2. Click and drag within the sequence viewer to select a region (e.g., select positions 100-150). You can use: `page.locator('.la-vz-seq').click({position: {x: 100, y: 20}}); page.locator('.la-vz-seq').click({position: {x: 150, y: 20}, modifiers: ['shift']});` or similar, depending on the SeqViz internals. Alternatively, triple-click on the sequence text area to select all, or use Cmd+A after clicking in the sequence. 3. Right-click on the selected sequence region to open the context menu (role="menu" should appear with data-testid="sequence-context-menu"). 4. Click the menu item labeled "Design primers here…" (use `page.getByRole("menuitem", {name: /Design primers here/i}).click()`). 5. Wait for the "Add primer" dialog to appear (verify with `page.locator('text=Add primer').isVisible()` or check for the textarea with placeholder "Type or paste bases"). 6. Click into the sequence textarea (first one you find) and fill it with "ATGGTGAGCAAGGGCGAGGAG". You can use `page.locator('textarea').first().fill("ATGGTGAGCAAGGGCGAGGAG")`. 7. Wait 700ms for the live stats (N-mer, GC%, Tm) to populate.
highlightLabel: The textarea labeled "Primer sequence (5' to 3')" - highlight for a how-to shot showing where to paste the primer sequence.
idealShot: The Add primer dialog centered on screen, showing: the dialog header with "Add primer" title, the "Type or paste" and "Mutagenesis" mode tabs (with "Type or paste" selected), the Primer name field, the Primer sequence textarea (filled with "ATGGTGAGCAAGGGCGAGGAG"), and the live stats panel below showing Length (21-mer), GC (57.1%), and Tm (59.5°C). The binding site section and alignment visualization should be visible if space allows, showing the primer's match on the template with blue matched bases and position/anneal info.
